places.name: Fouquet
places.description: Confectionery since 1852. Darker chocolate with ganache centers and fruit jellies.

places.name: Vandermeersch
places.description: Famous for Kougelhof cake and pain rustique bread.

places.name: Du Pain et des Idées
places.description: Known for Pain des amis and exceptional croissants.

places.name: Archibald
places.description: Organic breads. Specialty is Le Parisien, an einkorn loaf.

places.name: Pierre Hermé
places.description: Father of modern French pastry. Famous for macarons and the Ispahan flavor.

places.name: Poilâne
places.description: Iconic wood-oven bakery since 1932. Sourdough bread, apple tarts, and Punitions butter cookies.

places.name: Jean-Charles Rochoux
places.description: Chocolate sculptures and hazelnut praline paste.

places.name: Bague de Kenza
places.description: North African pastries made with fresh nuts and honey.

places.name: Le Chocolat Alain Ducasse
places.description: First bean-to-bar chocolate shop in Paris, from the famed chef.

places.name: La Maison du Chocolat
places.description: Classic Parisian chocolate shop. Celebrated for perfected ganache.

places.name: Jacques Génin
places.description: Buttery caramels. Tea salon with made-to-order pastries.

places.name: Chambelland
places.description: Gluten-free bakery using rice and buckwheat flours. Standout lemon tart.

places.name: Des Gâteaux et du Pain
places.description: Breads and pastries by a former Pierre Hermé pastry chef.

places.name: Patrick Roger
places.description: Intriguing flavors: lemongrass, saffron. Chocolate sculptures in the window.

places.name: Tout Autour du Pain
places.description: Award-winning baguette de tradition and Schwarzbrot bread.

places.name: Aux Merveilleux de Fred
places.description: Specializes in Merveilleux — meringue pastries layered with whipped cream.

places.name: La Pâtisserie Cyril Lignac
places.description: Chef Cyril Lignac's pastry shop. Éclairs, croissants, and pain aux céréales.

places.name: Berthillon
places.description: Legendary Parisian ice cream. Understated scoops and exceptional fruit sorbets.

places.name: Boulangerie Utopie
places.description: Poppy seed and sesame baguettes with a hint of curry.

places.name: Éric Kayser
places.description: Renowned baker. Known for pain aux graines and financiers.

places.name: Hugo & Victor
places.description: Most refined pâtisserie. Deconstructed French pastries with unusual flavors.

places.name: L'Éclair de Génie
places.description: All-éclair boutique with seasonal flavor variations.

places.name: Henri Le Roux
places.description: Salted butter caramel specialist, originally from Brittany.

places.name: Michel Chaudun
places.description: Chocolate Pavés (cobblestone-shaped) and disks with cocoa nibs.

places.name: Ladurée
places.description: Credited with inventing the Parisian macaron sandwich. Historic tea salon.

places.name: Sadaharu Aoki
places.description: Japanese pastry maker. Green tea galettes and cherry blossom éclairs.

places.name: Fou de Pâtisserie
places.description: Curates and sells pastries from many of the city's best bakers in one spot.

places.name: Le Boulanger de la Tour
places.description: Public bakery of the famous Tour d'Argent restaurant. Excellent baguettes.

places.name: Pain de Sucre
places.description: Marshmallows sold in jars and creative pastries by Didier Mathray.

places.name: Maison Georges Larnicol
places.description: Kouignettes — small kouign-amann pastries sold by weight.

places.name: Benoît Castel
places.description: Neighborhood pâtisserie. Far Breton and chocolate millefeuille.

places.name: Le Petit Grain
places.description: Natural fermentation. Contender for best croissant in Paris.

places.name: Sain Boulangerie
places.description: Chaussons aux pommes (apple turnovers) and naturally-leavened breads.

places.name: Maison Aleph
places.description: Middle Eastern-French fusion pastries. Madagascar vanilla flan.

places.name: The French Bastards
places.description: Lemon Meringue Tart and Cinnamon Babka. Hefty American-style pastries.

places.name: Tapisserie
places.description: From the Septime restaurant team. Tarte à l'Érable (maple syrup tart).

places.name: Plaq Chocolat
places.description: Bean-to-bar chocolate roasted on-site. Single-origin bars and chocolate sorbet.

places.name: François Pralus
places.description: Bean-to-bar. Famous for the Praluline, a brioche studded with pink pralines.

places.name: Maison Georges Larnicol (Saint-Germain)
places.description: Second location. Kouignettes — small kouign-amann pastries sold by weight.

places.name: Benoît Castel (Oberkampf)
places.description: Second location. Far Breton and chocolate millefeuille.

places.name: A La Biche au Bois
places.description: Game dishes, fixed menus, cheese course, and gargantuan salade Perigordine.

places.name: Au Passage
places.description: Fresh small plates for sharing. Roasted lamb shoulder standout.

places.name: Automne
places.description: One Michelin star. Seasonal tasting menus with fresh products.

places.name: Aux Bons Crus
places.description: Hearty French cooking — steak frites, tête de veau.

places.name: Baca'v
places.description: Urban auberge serving seasonal French fare.

places.name: Bellota-Bellota
places.description: Tapas bar with fabulous hams from wild acorn-fed pigs.

places.name: Breizh Café
places.description: Terrific crêpes and buckwheat galettes with Bordier butter.

places.name: Café les Deux Gares
places.description: Fresh oysters, tuna, mackerel, mussels in a restored café setting.

places.name: Café de la Nouvelle Mairie
places.description: Well-sourced food including seafood and housemade sausages. True local bistro.

places.name: Caluche
places.description: Daily-changing menu with remarkably fresh fare and natural wines.

places.name: Chez Dumonet
places.description: Reliably good bistro — duck confit and Grand Marnier soufflé.

places.name: Chez Georges
places.description: Classic French bistro. Escargots, steak frites, sole meunière.

places.name: Chez Omar
places.description: North African couscous, steak-frites, lamb brochettes. Open every day.

places.name: Chez Paul
places.description: Côte de Boeuf, duck confit. One of the true remaining bistros.

places.name: Comer
places.description: Excellent Mexican cuisine using fresh French ingredients. Vegan-friendly.

places.name: Comice
places.description: Husband-chef and wife-sommelier team. Exquisite fixed menu dining.

places.name: Frenchie Wine Bar
places.description: Small plates with excellent food. Wine bar arm of famed Frenchie.

places.name: Grande Brasserie
places.description: Classic brasserie — oeuf mayonnaise, oysters, pâté en croûte. Open daily.

places.name: Holybelly
places.description: Breakfast and lunch with the freshest ingredients and locally roasted coffee.

places.name: Jones
places.description: Wine bar/restaurant with creative dishes like veal milanese.

places.name: Juveniles
places.description: Intimate wine bar and Paris fixture. Daily-changing menu with terrines.

places.name: Kubri
places.description: Lebanese cuisine by cheffe Rita Higgins. Daily-changing menu. Standout roast chicken.

places.name: La Buvette
places.description: Small casual wine bar with natural wines, housemade terrine, and giant beans.

places.name: L'Apibo
places.description: Contemporary bistronomique cooking with especially good desserts.

places.name: L'Avant Comptoir de la Mer
places.description: Seafood stand-up wine bar — oysters, ceviche, salted butter at the bar.

places.name: Le Bambou
places.description: Bustling, inexpensive, authentic Vietnamese food.

places.name: Le Bon Georges
places.description: Classic bistro with well-sourced meats. Steak tartare, steak frites.

places.name: Le Boui-Boui
places.description: Hearty Auvergne cuisine. Housemade terrine. A real French restaurant.

places.name: Le Clarence
places.description: Two Michelin stars. Modern French with seafood focus in a 19th-century hôtel particulier.

places.name: Le Garde Robe
places.description: Natural wines with charcuterie and cheeses. Friendly staff.

places.name: Le Grand Bain
places.description: Daily specials and small plates for sharing. Natural wines in Belleville.

places.name: Le Petit Vendôme
places.description: Arguably the best sandwiches in Paris, particularly the jambon-beurre.

places.name: Le Relais de l'Entrecôte
places.description: One thing only — entrecôte and fries with tableside sauce. No reservations.

places.name: Le Rigmarole
places.description: French ingredients with Japanese touch. Grilled meats. Lunch only.

places.name: Le Rubis
places.description: Authentic Parisian wine bar with rustic lunch and charcuterie.

places.name: Le Saint-Sébastien
places.description: Modern, carefully-crafted French cooking with seasonal menu.

places.name: Le Verre Volé
places.description: Fresh modern French cooking. Wine bottles lining the shelves.

places.name: Les Arlots
places.description: Daily-changing menu. Entrecôte, sausage and mash.

places.name: Little Breizh
places.description: Buckwheat galettes with ham, cheese, eggs. Also a sweet crêperie.

places.name: Oxymore
places.description: French pizzeria with decidedly French toppings.

places.name: Parcelles
places.description: Pressed pork terrine, fried sweetbreads, excellent wine list.

places.name: Poulette
places.description: Turn-of-the-century restoration. Cuisine maison including steak-frites and quail.

places.name: Recoin
places.description: Neighborhood bistro with inventive food and daily-changing menu.

places.name: Saravanaa Bhavan
places.description: Completely vegetarian Indian restaurant. Very good food.

places.name: Septime
places.description: Nordic-inspired cuisine by chef Bertrand Grébaut. Sparkling fresh ingredients.

places.name: Verjus
places.description: Inventive outstanding cuisine. Daily-changing multi-course menus. Casual wine bar downstairs.
